desc: Samarkand is one of Central Asia's great Silk Road cities, celebrated for blue-tiled madrasas, Timurid monuments, and layered caravan-route history. It is strongest for architecture-first itineraries in spring and autumn, when temperatures are more manageable for long outdoor heritage circuits.

todo.name: Registan ensemble visit
todo.desc: Buy entry as gates open and plan 1.5-2 hours across all three madrasas, then return after sunset for illuminated façades and lighter crowds.

todo.name: Shah-i-Zinda necropolis walk
todo.desc: Arrive before coach groups (usually before 09:30), wear shoulders-and-knees-covering clothing, and allow slow uphill walking on uneven steps.

todo.name: Gur-e-Amir Mausoleum
todo.desc: Pair this stop with Registan in the same district and visit near dusk when tile colors are strongest and daytime heat drops.

todo.name: Bibi-Khanym Mosque and Siab Bazaar
todo.desc: Go early: bazaar produce activity is best before noon, and the mosque forecourt has minimal shade later in the day.

todo.name: Ulugh Beg Observatory
todo.desc: Use a taxi app or prearranged driver for the short uphill transfer from central Samarkand, then budget 45-60 minutes for museum and site.

todo.name: Traditional plov dinner and folk show
todo.desc: Reserve at least 24-48 hours ahead for popular folklore restaurants, especially on Thursday-Saturday evenings when local groups book large tables.

wifi.note: Adequate in central hotels and cafés, with mobile data useful for translation, ride-hailing, and day-trip routing.

prac.fltNote: No regular Gatwick-Samarkand nonstop service; common routings connect via Istanbul, Abu Dhabi, or Tashkent. Return fares are often ~£260-£450 off-peak, ~£450-£650 in shoulder demand, and ~£650+ around peak holiday windows.
prac.visa: Uzbekistan allows visa-free entry for many nationalities including UK and EU for short stays; verify current permitted duration before departure.
alerts: Registan, Shah-i-Zinda, and Gur-e-Amir ticket lines are longest in April-May and September-October; start at opening time for cooler conditions and shorter waits.
Summer daytime heat regularly pushes above 35°C, making exposed plaza visits uncomfortable from midday to late afternoon.
Afrosiyob high-speed train seats between Tashkent and Samarkand can sell out several days ahead around holidays, so secure rail tickets as soon as dates are fixed.

sweet: April-early June and September-October are usually best for Silk Road sightseeing in manageable temperatures.
